text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously constructive - inflation expectations easing (Kalshi traders see energy-driven peak in May), Iran deal optimism pulling oil lower, which typically lifts growth/tech multiples. That's the real driver for your chip-heavy book today.

Your holdings: SOXL (-11.93%) is the loudest problem - it's a 3x leveraged ETF that decays daily, and on margin that double-leverage effect can compound losses fast; stop is $200, price is $224, not broken yet but watch it closely. SPAL (-8.47%) is a brand-new, very volatile leveraged IPO - already down nearly 9%, sitting uncomfortably close to its $21.38 stop; margin amplifies any further drop hard. PTNM (-15.25%) is a tiny spec down sharply - tiny float, thin liquidity, and no clear catalyst visible today.

Watch: Whether SOXL holds above $200 stop as chips digest recent moves. Also watch if easing inflation narrative gets confirmed data - that's the fuel your whole book needs.

Bottom line: Your leveraged and speculative positions are where the pain lives - protect capital there before thinking about anything new.
plays: SOXL: Stop is $200 - honor it. If price breaks below that level, exit; no reason to ride a 3x decay product through a breakdown on margin.
SPAL: At $23.02 with stop $21.38, you have roughly 7% of cushion on a fast-moving leveraged IPO. Consider reducing size now if the position is oversized relative to your margin exposure.
No new buys today - chip setups like AMAT and KLAC are in premium (extended), and without a fresh catalyst specific to those names, chasing here adds risk to an already leveraged book.
